controls.control_objective: To ensure be called the reserve bank of india (regional rural banks - cash reserve ratio and statutory liquidity ratio) directions, 2025
controls.control_description:
These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Regional Rural Banks
- Cash Reserve Ratio and Statutory Liquidity Ratio) Directions, 2025.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be called the reserve bank of india (regional rural banks - cash reserve ratio and statutory liquidity ratio) directions, 2025

controls.control_objective: To ensure come into force with immediate effect
controls.control_description: These Directions shall come into force with immediate effect.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to come into force with immediate effect

controls.control_objective: To ensure be applicable to regional rural banks (hereinafter collectively referred to as 'banks' or 'rrbs' and individually as a 'bank' or 'rrb')
controls.control_description:
These Directions shall be applicable to Regional Rural Banks (hereinafter
collectively referred to as 'banks' or 'RRBs' and individually as a 'bank' or 'RRB').
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be applicable to regional rural banks (hereinafter collectively referred to as 'banks' or 'rrbs' and individually as a 'bank' or 'rrb')

controls.control_objective: To ensure be reported to the rbi under the statutory return form a
controls.control_description:
The maintenance of Cash Reserve Ratio (CRR) shall be reported to the RBI under
the statutory return Form A.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be reported to the rbi under the statutory return form a

controls.control_objective: To ensure be reported to the rbi under the statutory return form viii
controls.control_description:
The maintenance of Statutory Liquidity Ratio (SLR) shall be reported to the RBI
under the statutory return Form VIII.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be reported to the rbi under the statutory return form viii

controls.control_objective: To ensure bear the meaning assigned to them below: (1) 'aggregate deposits' shall mean aggregation of demand and time deposits
controls.control_description:
In these Directions, unless the context otherwise requires, the terms herein shall
bear the meaning assigned to them below:
(1) 'Aggregate Deposits' shall mean aggregation of demand and time deposits.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to bear the meaning assigned to them below: (1) 'aggregate deposits' shall mean aggregation of demand and time deposits

controls.control_objective: To ensure undertake the apportionment of saving bank account into demand liability and time liability as per the following procedure: explanation: explanation:
controls.control_description:
(2) 'Apportionment of Saving Bank Account into demand liability and time liability': a
bank shall undertake the apportionment of Saving Bank Account into demand liability
and time liability as per the following procedure:
Explanation:
Explanation: Money at Call and Short Notice from outside the banking system shall
be shown against liability to others.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to undertake the apportionment of saving bank account into demand liability and time liability as per the following procedure: explanation: explanation:

controls.control_objective: To ensure the period from the first day to the fifteenth day of each calendar month or sixteenth day to the last day of each calendar month, both days inclusive
controls.control_description:
(14) 2'Fortnight' means the period from the first day to the fifteenth day of each
calendar month or sixteenth day to the last day of each calendar month, both days
inclusive.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to the period from the first day to the fifteenth day of each calendar month or sixteenth day to the last day of each calendar month, both days inclusive

controls.control_objective: To ensure consist of investment in approved government securities and other approved securities (as explained below)
controls.control_description:
(15) 'Investment in India' shall consist of investment in approved government
securities and other approved securities (as explained below).
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to consist of investment in approved government securities and other approved securities (as explained below)

controls.control_objective: To ensure include both encumbered and unencumbered securities as per the bank's investment book
controls.control_description:
These shall include
both encumbered and unencumbered securities as per the bank's investment book.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to include both encumbered and unencumbered securities as per the bank's investment book

controls.control_objective: To ensure mean investment in government securities which are not approved securities (such as sdls issued as uday bonds)
controls.control_description:
(16) 'Investment in India in other Government Securities' shall mean Investment in
Government securities which are not approved securities (such as SDLs issued as
UDAY bonds).
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to mean investment in government securities which are not approved securities (such as sdls issued as uday bonds)

controls.control_objective: To ensure mean fixed and variable rate repo operations (for injection of liquidity) and reverse repo operations (for absorption of liquidity) conducted by the r
controls.control_description:
(17) 'Liquidity Adjustment Facility (LAF)' shall mean fixed and variable rate Repo
operations (for injection of liquidity) and reverse repo operations (for absorption of
liquidity) conducted by the Reserve Bank of India from time to time.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to mean fixed and variable rate repo operations (for injection of liquidity) and reverse repo operations (for absorption of liquidity) conducted by the r

controls.control_objective: To ensure mean the facility under which the eligible banks can avail liquidity support from the reserve bank against excess slr holdings
controls.control_description:
(18) 'Marginal standing facility' shall mean the facility under which the eligible banks
can avail liquidity support from the Reserve Bank against excess SLR holdings.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to mean the facility under which the eligible banks can avail liquidity support from the reserve bank against excess slr holdings

controls.control_objective: To ensure mean government securities, other than the securities mentioned in paragraph 6(3) above
controls.control_description:
(21) 'Other Approved Securities' shall mean Government Securities, other than the
securities mentioned in paragraph 6(3) above, subject to the condition that they are
notified as approved securities.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to mean government securities, other than the securities mentioned in paragraph 6(3) above

controls.control_objective: To ensure include the following: explanation:
controls.control_description:
(22) 'Other Demand and Time Liabilities (ODTL)' shall include the following:
Explanation:
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to include the following: explanation:

controls.control_objective: To ensure to calculate the proportion of its savings bank deposits as at the close of business on march 31 and september 30, into demand and time liabilities in
controls.control_description:
(i) A bank is required to calculate the proportion of its savings bank deposits as at
the close of business on March 31 and September 30, into demand and time
liabilities in terms of Regulation 7 of The Reserve Bank of India Scheduled
Banks' Regulations, 1951.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to to calculate the proportion of its savings bank deposits as at the close of business on march 31 and september 30, into demand and time liabilities in

controls.control_objective: To ensure be treated by the bank as the amount representing the "time liability" portion of the savings bank deposits
controls.control_description:
(ii) The average of the minimum balances maintained (in each account) in each of
the months during the half year period shall be treated by the bank as the amount
representing the "time liability" portion of the savings bank deposits.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be treated by the bank as the amount representing the "time liability" portion of the savings bank deposits

controls.control_objective: To ensure be applied for arriving at demand and time liabilities components of savings bank deposits for all reporting fortnights during the next half year
controls.control_description:
(iii) The proportions of demand and time liabilities so obtained for each half year
shall be applied for arriving at demand and time liabilities components of savings
bank deposits for all reporting fortnights during the next half year.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be applied for arriving at demand and time liabilities components of savings bank deposits for all reporting fortnights during the next half year

controls.control_objective: To ensure be considered as approved securities (approved securities are commonly known as slr securities):
controls.control_description:
(3) 'Approved Securities/SLR securities': Following securities shall be considered as
approved securities (approved securities are commonly known as SLR securities):
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be considered as approved securities (approved securities are commonly known as slr securities):

controls.control_objective: To ensure be notified by the reserve bank of india (as and when prescribed)
controls.control_description:
(iv) Any other instrument as may be notified by the Reserve Bank of India (as and
when prescribed).
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be notified by the reserve bank of india (as and when prescribed)

controls.control_objective: To ensure maintain in india by way of cash reserve, a sum equivalent to such percent of the total of its ndtl in india, in such manner and for such dates, as th
controls.control_description:
Every RRB shall maintain in India by way of cash reserve, a sum equivalent to
such percent of the total of its NDTL in India, in such manner and for such dates, as
the Reserve Bank in terms of Section 42(1) of the RBI Act, 1934 and Section 18(1) of
BR Act, 1949, may specify, by notification in the Official Gazette, from time to time
having regard to the needs of securing the monetary stability in the country.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to maintain in india by way of cash reserve, a sum equivalent to such percent of the total of its ndtl in india, in such manner and for such dates, as th

controls.control_objective: To ensure require the
controls.control_description: In terms of Section 42(1A) of RBI Act, 1934, the Reserve Bank may require the
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to require the

controls.control_objective: To ensure be less than the rate specified by the reserve bank in the notification published in the gazette of india from time to time
controls.control_description:
scheduled banks to maintain, in addition to the balances prescribed under Section
42(1) of the Act, an additional average daily balance, the amount of which shall not be
less than the rate specified by the Reserve Bank in the notification published in the
Gazette of India from time to time.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be less than the rate specified by the reserve bank in the notification published in the gazette of india from time to time

controls.control_objective: To ensure be calculated with reference to the excess of the total of ndtl of the bank as shown in the returns referred to in section 42(2) of the rbi act, 1934
controls.control_description:
Provided that such additional balance shall be calculated with reference to the excess
of the total of NDTL of the bank as shown in the Returns referred to in Section 42(2)
of the RBI Act, 1934 over the total of its NDTL at the close of the business on the date
specified in the notification.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be calculated with reference to the excess of the total of ndtl of the bank as shown in the returns referred to in section 42(2) of the rbi act, 1934

controls.control_objective: To ensure be less than 3
controls.control_description:
Every scheduled bank shall maintain in India with the Reserve Bank, an average
daily balance, the amount of which shall not be less than 3.75 per cent, 3.5 per cent,
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be less than 3

controls.control_objective: To ensure be less than the crr prescribed by the reserve bank
controls.control_description:
Every scheduled bank shall maintain minimum CRR of not less than 90 percent of
the required CRR on all days during the reporting fortnight, in such a manner that the
average of CRR maintained daily shall not be less than the CRR prescribed by the
Reserve Bank.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be less than the crr prescribed by the reserve bank

controls.control_objective: To ensure be regarded as liability in india of a bank
controls.control_description:
For the purpose of these Directions, the Reserve Bank may specify from time to
time with reference to any transaction or class of transactions that such transaction or
transactions shall be regarded as liability in India of a bank.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be regarded as liability in india of a bank

controls.control_objective: To ensure be regarded, for the purpose of these directions, as liability in india of a bank, the bank shall approach the rbi
controls.control_description:
If any question arises as to whether any transaction or transactions shall be
regarded, for the purpose of these Directions, as liability in India of a bank, the bank
shall approach the RBI.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be regarded, for the purpose of these directions, as liability in india of a bank, the bank shall approach the rbi

controls.control_objective: To ensure be final
controls.control_description: The decision of the Reserve Bank thereon shall be final.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be final

controls.control_objective: To ensure be reckoned as 'liabilities to others' and shall be subject to reserve requirements
controls.control_description:
Loans / borrowings from abroad by banks in India shall be reckoned as 'liabilities
to others' and shall be subject to reserve requirements.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be reckoned as 'liabilities to others' and shall be subject to reserve requirements

controls.control_objective: To ensure be reckoned as liability for the computation of ndtl for the purpose of reserve requirements
controls.control_description:
Upper Tier II instruments raised and maintained in India / abroad shall be reckoned
as liability for the computation of NDTL for the purpose of reserve requirements.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be reckoned as liability for the computation of ndtl for the purpose of reserve requirements

controls.control_objective: To ensure be reckoned as 'liability to others in india' for the computation of ndtl
controls.control_description:
The balance amount in respect of the drafts issued by the accepting bank on its
correspondent bank under the Remittance Facilities Scheme and remaining unpaid
shall be reckoned as 'Liability to others in India' for the computation of NDTL.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be reckoned as 'liability to others in india' for the computation of ndtl

controls.control_objective: To ensure be reckoned as 'liability to the banking system' and this liability may be netted off by the correspondent banks against the inter-bank assets
controls.control_description:
The
amount received by correspondent banks shall be reckoned as 'Liability to the Banking
System' and this liability may be netted off by the correspondent banks against the
inter-bank assets.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be reckoned as 'liability to the banking system' and this liability may be netted off by the correspondent banks against the inter-bank assets

controls.control_objective: To ensure be treated as 'assets with banking system' and banks shall have the option to net them off from their inter-bank liabilities
controls.control_description:
Sums placed by banks for issuing drafts / interest / dividend warrants shall be
treated as 'Assets with banking system' and banks shall have the option to net them
off from their inter-bank liabilities.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be treated as 'assets with banking system' and banks shall have the option to net them off from their inter-bank liabilities

controls.control_objective: To ensure continue with interest application on savings bank deposits on a daily product basis
controls.control_description:
scheduled banks in respect of their savings bank deposits on the basis of the position
as at the close of business on 30th September and 31st March every year shall
continue with interest application on savings bank deposits on a daily product basis.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to continue with interest application on savings bank deposits on a daily product basis

controls.control_objective: To ensure form part of liabilities of a bank for the purpose of crr and slr: (1) paid up capital, reserves, borrowings through instruments qualifying for tier 1
controls.control_description:
The liabilities mentioned below shall not form part of liabilities of a bank for the
purpose of CRR and SLR:
(1) Paid up capital, reserves, borrowings through instruments qualifying for Tier 1 and
<image>
additional Tier 1 capital;
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to form part of liabilities of a bank for the purpose of crr and slr: (1) paid up capital, reserves, borrowings through instruments qualifying for tier 1

controls.control_objective: To ensure have to be taken into account for the purpose of calculation of reserve requirements
controls.control_description:
Provided that the funds collected by various branches of the bank or other banks for
the issue and held pending finalisation of allotment of the additional Tier 1 preference
shares shall have to be taken into account for the purpose of calculation of reserve
requirements.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to have to be taken into account for the purpose of calculation of reserve requirements

controls.control_objective: To ensure be reduced by the aggregate of the liabilities of all such banks and institutions to the scheduled bank
controls.control_description:
(f) any other financial institution notified by the Central Government in this behalf
shall be reduced by the aggregate of the liabilities of all such banks and
institutions to the scheduled bank.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be reduced by the aggregate of the liabilities of all such banks and institutions to the scheduled bank

controls.control_objective: To ensure be included as part of bank credit for the purpose of these directions
controls.control_description:
Loans out of Foreign Currency Non-Resident Accounts (Banks), (FCNR [B]
Deposits Scheme) and Inter-Bank Foreign Currency (IBFC) deposits shall be included
as part of bank credit for the purpose of these Directions.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be included as part of bank credit for the purpose of these directions

controls.control_objective: To ensure use the conversion rate announced by financial benchmarks india private limited (fbil) for the purpose of converting foreign assets / liabilities for
controls.control_description:
Banks shall use the
conversion rate announced by Financial Benchmarks India Private Limited (FBIL) for
the purpose of converting foreign assets / liabilities for reporting in the statutory returns
mentioned at paragraphs 4 and 5 of these directions.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to use the conversion rate announced by financial benchmarks india private limited (fbil) for the purpose of converting foreign assets / liabilities for

controls.control_objective: To ensure use new york closing rate pertaining to the day end of the reporting day for converting such currencies into usd and then use the reference rate of fb
controls.control_description:
For conversion of assets /
liabilities in other currencies, banks may use New York Closing Rate pertaining to the
day end of the reporting day for converting such currencies into USD and then use the
reference rate of FBIL for USD/INR for the same day for conversion into INR.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to use new york closing rate pertaining to the day end of the reporting day for converting such currencies into usd and then use the reference rate of fb

controls.control_objective: To ensure be less than such percentage not exceeding 40 percent of the total of its demand and time liabilities in india as on the last day of the second preced
controls.control_description:
Every bank, in addition to the cash reserves which it is required to maintain under
these Directions, shall maintain in India, assets, the value of which shall not be less
than such percentage not exceeding 40 percent of the total of its demand and time
liabilities in India as on the last day of the second preceding fortnight as the Reserve
Bank may, by notification in the Official Gazette, specify from time to time and such
assets shall be maintained in such form and such manner, as may be specified in such
notification.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be less than such percentage not exceeding 40 percent of the total of its demand and time liabilities in india as on the last day of the second preced

controls.control_objective: To ensure , at the close of business on any day, be less than 18 per cent of its total net demand and time liabilities in india as on the last day of the second
controls.control_description:
Every RRB shall maintain in India assets (hereinafter referred to as 'SLR assets')
the value of which shall not, at the close of business on any day, be less than 18 per
cent of its total net demand and time liabilities in India as on the last day of the second
preceding fortnight in accordance with the method of valuation specified by RBI from
time to time.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to , at the close of business on any day, be less than 18 per cent of its total net demand and time liabilities in india as on the last day of the second

controls.control_objective: To ensure have the option to participate in the marginal standing facility (msf) scheme introduced by the reserve bank
controls.control_description:
Banks permitted by Reserve Bank shall have the option to participate in the
Marginal Standing Facility (MSF) Scheme introduced by the Reserve Bank.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to have the option to participate in the marginal standing facility (msf) scheme introduced by the reserve bank

controls.control_objective: To ensure have the option to borrow up to two percent of their respective ndtl outstanding at the end of the second preceding fortnight
controls.control_description:
The
features of the scheme are:
(1) The eligible banks shall have the option to borrow up to two percent of their
respective NDTL outstanding at the end of the second preceding fortnight.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to have the option to borrow up to two percent of their respective ndtl outstanding at the end of the second preceding fortnight

controls.control_objective: To ensure also continue to access overnight funds under this facility against their excess slr holdings
controls.control_description:
(2) The eligible entities shall also continue to access overnight funds under this facility
against their excess SLR holdings.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to also continue to access overnight funds under this facility against their excess slr holdings

controls.control_objective: To ensure have the obligation to seek a specific waiver for default in slr compliance arising out of use of this facility in terms of notification issued under
controls.control_description:
(3) In the event of a bank's SLR holding falling below the statutory requirement up to
two percent of its NDTL, the bank shall not have the obligation to seek a specific waiver
for default in SLR compliance arising out of use of this facility in terms of notification
issued under sub section (2A) of Section 24 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to have the obligation to seek a specific waiver for default in slr compliance arising out of use of this facility in terms of notification issued under

controls.control_objective: To ensure be maintained by rrbs as under: (1) cash, or
controls.control_description:
SLR assets shall be maintained by RRBs as under:
(1) Cash, or;
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be maintained by rrbs as under: (1) cash, or

controls.control_objective: To ensure be considered as eligible assets for slr maintenance
controls.control_description:
Provided that the instruments referred to in items (3)(i) to (3)(iii) mentioned above,
that have been acquired from Reserve Bank of India under reverse repo, shall be
considered as eligible assets for SLR maintenance.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be considered as eligible assets for slr maintenance

controls.control_objective: To ensure be treated as encumbered for the purpose of maintenance of slr assets, namely:
controls.control_description:
Provided further that the following SLR-securities shall not be treated as
encumbered for the purpose of maintenance of SLR assets, namely:
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be treated as encumbered for the purpose of maintenance of slr assets, namely:

controls.control_objective: To ensure be exempted from crr / slr computation and the security acquired under repo shall be eligible for slr provided the security is primarily eligible for
controls.control_description:
(ii) Funds borrowed under repo including tri-party repo in government securities
shall be exempted from CRR / SLR computation and the security acquired under
repo shall be eligible for SLR provided the security is primarily eligible for SLR
as per the provisions of the Act under which it is required to be maintained.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be exempted from crr / slr computation and the security acquired under repo shall be eligible for slr provided the security is primarily eligible for

controls.control_objective: To ensure be reckoned as liabilities for cash reserve ratio / statutory liquidity ratio requirement and, to the extent these liabilities are to the banking syst
controls.control_description:
(iii) Borrowings by a bank through repo in corporate bonds and debentures shall be
reckoned as liabilities for Cash Reserve Ratio / Statutory Liquidity Ratio
requirement and, to the extent these liabilities are to the banking system, they
shall be netted as per Section 42 (1) (d) of the RBI Act, 1934.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be reckoned as liabilities for cash reserve ratio / statutory liquidity ratio requirement and, to the extent these liabilities are to the banking syst

controls.control_objective: To ensure maintain investments in government securities only in subsidiary general ledger (sgl) accounts with reserve bank or in csgl accounts of scheduled comm
controls.control_description:
(iv) All banks shall maintain investments in Government Securities only in
Subsidiary General Ledger (SGL) Accounts with Reserve Bank or in CSGL
Accounts of scheduled commercial banks, Primary Dealers (PDs), State Co-
operative Banks, and Stock Holding Corporation of India Ltd.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to maintain investments in government securities only in subsidiary general ledger (sgl) accounts with reserve bank or in csgl accounts of scheduled comm

controls.control_objective: To ensure report the sdf balances held by banks with rbi in form viii, as it is an eligible asset for slr maintenance
controls.control_description:
(v) 6Banks shall report the SDF balances held by banks with RBI in Form VIII, as it
is an eligible asset for SLR maintenance.
controls.implementation_method: Implement reporting procedure to report the sdf balances held by banks with rbi in form viii, as it is an eligible asset for slr maintenance

controls.control_objective: To ensure be eligible for cash reserve ratio (crr) maintenance
controls.control_description:
The balances held by banks with RBI
under the SDF shall not be eligible for Cash Reserve Ratio (CRR) maintenance.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be eligible for cash reserve ratio (crr) maintenance

controls.control_objective: To ensure be treated as government of india treasury bill and thus be reckoned as slr security
controls.control_description:
(ii) The cash management bill shall be treated as Government of India Treasury
Bill and thus be reckoned as SLR security.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be treated as government of india treasury bill and thus be reckoned as slr security

controls.control_objective: To ensure be as below: (1) total ndtl for the purpose of slr under section 24 (2a) of banking regulation act, 1949, shall be computed on the similar procedure a
controls.control_description:
The procedure for computation of NDTL for SLR shall be as below:
(1) Total NDTL for the purpose of SLR under Section 24 (2A) of Banking Regulation
Act, 1949, shall be computed on the similar procedure as followed for CRR.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be as below: (1) total ndtl for the purpose of slr under section 24 (2a) of banking regulation act, 1949, shall be computed on the similar procedure a

controls.control_objective: To ensure form
controls.control_description: (2) The liabilities mentioned under paragraph 19 of these Directions shall not form
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to form

controls.control_objective: To ensure to include inter-bank term deposits / term borrowing liabilities of all maturities in 'liabilities to the banking system'
controls.control_description:
(3) SCBs are required to include inter-bank term deposits / term borrowing liabilities
of all maturities in 'Liabilities to the Banking System'.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to to include inter-bank term deposits / term borrowing liabilities of all maturities in 'liabilities to the banking system'

controls.control_objective: To ensure include their inter-bank assets of term deposits and term lending of all maturities in 'assets with the banking system' for computation of ndtl for sl
controls.control_description:
(4) Banks shall include their inter-bank assets of term deposits and term lending of
all maturities in 'Assets with the Banking System' for computation of NDTL for SLR
purpose.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to include their inter-bank assets of term deposits and term lending of all maturities in 'assets with the banking system' for computation of ndtl for sl

controls.control_objective: To ensure be in accordance with the extant instructions in reserve bank of india (regional rural banks - classification, valuation and operation of investment p
controls.control_description:
Classification and valuation of approved securities shall be in accordance with the
extant instructions in Reserve Bank of India (Regional Rural Banks - Classification,
Valuation and Operation of Investment Portfolio) Directions, 2025, as applicable.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be in accordance with the extant instructions in reserve bank of india (regional rural banks - classification, valuation and operation of investment p

controls.control_objective: To ensure submit to reserve bank a return in form 'a' (annex i), at the close of business on 9 last day of each fortnight within five days after the date of the
controls.control_description:
Under Section 42(2) of the RBI Act, 1934, every RRB shall submit to Reserve
Bank a Return in Form 'A' (Annex I), at the close of business on 9 last day of each
fortnight within five days after the date of the relevant fortnight to which it relates.
controls.implementation_method: Implement reporting procedure to submit to reserve bank a return in form 'a' (annex i), at the close of business on 9 last day of each fortnight within five days after the date of the

controls.control_objective: To ensure give at the close of business of the preceding working day's figure in respect of such office or offices, but shall nevertheless be deemed to relate t
controls.control_description:
Where such last day of each fortnight is a public holiday under the Negotiable
Instruments Act, 1881, for one or more offices of the bank, the Return shall give at the
close of business of the preceding working day's figure in respect of such office or
offices, but shall nevertheless be deemed to relate to last day of such fortnight.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to give at the close of business of the preceding working day's figure in respect of such office or offices, but shall nevertheless be deemed to relate t

controls.control_objective: To ensure be submitted to reserve bank along with: (1) the memorandum to the return in form 'a' giving details of paid-up capital, reserves, time deposits compr
controls.control_description:
The Return in Form 'A', shall be submitted to Reserve Bank along with:
(1) the Memorandum to the Return in Form 'A' giving details of paid-up capital,
reserves, time deposits comprising short-term (of contractual maturity of one year or
less) and long-term (of contractual maturity of more than one year), certificates of
deposits, NDTL, total CRR requirement, etc.,
(2) Annex A to the Return in Form 'A' showing all foreign currency liabilities and
assets, and
(3) Annex B to return in Form 'A' giving details about investment in approved
securities, investment in unapproved securities, memo items such as subscription to
shares / debentures / bonds in primary market and subscriptions through private
placement.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be submitted to reserve bank along with: (1) the memorandum to the return in form 'a' giving details of paid-up capital, reserves, time deposits compr

controls.control_objective: To ensure adhere to the following practice for presentation of reverse repo transactions in the form 'a' return: (1) reverse repo transactions with the banks sh
controls.control_description:
The RRB should adhere to the following practice for presentation of Reverse Repo
transactions in the Form 'A' return:
(1) Reverse Repo transactions with the banks should be reported as under:
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to adhere to the following practice for presentation of reverse repo transactions in the form 'a' return: (1) reverse repo transactions with the banks sh

controls.control_objective: To ensure be reported as under:
controls.control_description:
(b) Memo item 2.1 and 2.2 of Annex A to Form A (i.e., under Inter Bank Assets)
(2) The Reverse Repo transactions of a bank with non-banks (other institutions) should
be reported as under:
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be reported as under:

controls.control_objective: To ensure give reasons therefor in the return
controls.control_description:
Whenever there are wide variations between the sources and uses of funds as
being reported in the fortnightly Return and the variations exceed 20 percent, the
banks concerned should give reasons therefor in the Return.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to give reasons therefor in the return

controls.control_objective: To ensure to furnish a list of the names, the officials designations and specimen signatures of the officers of the banks who are authorised to sign on behalf o
controls.control_description:
In terms of Regulation 5(i) (c) of the Scheduled Banks Regulations,1951, the
banks are required to furnish a list of the names, the officials designations and
specimen signatures of the officers of the banks who are authorised to sign on behalf
of the banks, Returns prescribed under Section 42(2) of the RBI Act, 1934, and Section
18 and 24 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949.
controls.implementation_method: Implement reporting procedure to to furnish a list of the names, the officials designations and specimen signatures of the officers of the banks who are authorised to sign on behalf o

controls.control_objective: To ensure be no provisional or final or special form a return
controls.control_description:
11Under the new reporting structure, as amended vide Banking Laws
(Amendment) Act 2025, there shall be no Provisional or Final or Special Form A return.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be no provisional or final or special form a return

controls.control_objective: To ensure to submit single form a return
controls.control_description: The banks are required to submit single Form A return.
controls.implementation_method: Implement reporting procedure to to submit single form a return

controls.control_objective: To ensure to submit fortnightly form a return from december 15, 2025 and monthly form viii return from december 2025 on the cims portal
controls.control_description:
The banks are required to submit
fortnightly Form A return from December 15, 2025 and monthly Form VIII return from
December 2025 on the CIMS portal.Form A and Form VIII are not to be submitted in
hard copy / paper returns by RRBs.
controls.implementation_method: Implement reporting procedure to to submit fortnightly form a return from december 15, 2025 and monthly form viii return from december 2025 on the cims portal

controls.control_objective: To ensure be done, as presently applicable, i
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be done, as presently applicable, i

controls.control_objective: To ensure maintain minimum crr of not less than hundred per cent of the required crr during the transition period
controls.control_description:
Further, the banks shall
maintain minimum CRR of not less than hundred per cent of the required CRR during
the transition period.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to maintain minimum crr of not less than hundred per cent of the required crr during the transition period

controls.control_objective: To ensure also submit form a return for december 12, 2025, as per extant reporting structure
controls.control_description:
The banks shall also submit Form A return for December 12,
2025, as per extant reporting structure.
controls.implementation_method: Implement reporting procedure to also submit form a return for december 12, 2025, as per extant reporting structure

controls.control_objective: To ensure submit to the reserve bank before 20th day of every month, a return in form viii (annex ii) showing the amount of slr held on last day of each fortnig
controls.control_description:
Every RRB shall submit to the Reserve Bank before 20th day of every month, a
Return in Form VIII (Annex II) showing the amount of SLR held on last day of each
fortnight during the immediate preceding month with particulars of their DTL in India
held on such day or if any such day is a public holiday under the Negotiable
Instruments Act, 1881, at the close of business on the preceding working day.
controls.implementation_method: Implement reporting procedure to submit to the reserve bank before 20th day of every month, a return in form viii (annex ii) showing the amount of slr held on last day of each fortnig

controls.control_objective: To ensure also submit a statement as annex to form viii return giving daily position of (a) assets held for the purpose of compliance with slr
controls.control_description:
Every scheduled bank shall also submit a statement as Annex to Form VIII Return
giving daily position of (a) assets held for the purpose of compliance with SLR,
controls.implementation_method: Implement reporting procedure to also submit a statement as annex to form viii return giving daily position of (a) assets held for the purpose of compliance with slr

controls.control_objective: To ensure verify and certify that all items of outside liabilities, as per the bank's books had been duly compiled by the bank and correctly reflected under 12
controls.control_description:
The Statutory Auditors shall verify and certify that all items of outside liabilities, as
per the bank's books had been duly compiled by the bank and correctly reflected under
12 Inserted with effect from December 15, 2025 vide Reserve Bank of India (Regional Rural Banks - Cash Reserve Ratio and Statutory Liquidity Ratio) (Amendment) Directions, 2025 dated December 11, 2025 13 Inserted with effect from December 12, 2025 vide Reserve Bank of India (Regional Rural Banks - Cash Reserve Ratio and Statutory Liquidity Ratio) (Amendment) Directions, 2025 dated December 11, 2025
<image>
NDTL in the fortnightly / monthly statutory returns submitted to Reserve Bank for the
financial year.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to verify and certify that all items of outside liabilities, as per the bank's books had been duly compiled by the bank and correctly reflected under 12

controls.control_objective: To ensure be recovered from rrbs in the event of shortfall in maintenance of prescribed crr on a daily basis for that day at the rate of three percent per annum
controls.control_description:
(1) Penal interest shall be recovered from RRBs in the event of shortfall in
maintenance of prescribed CRR on a daily basis for that day at the rate of three
percent per annum above the Bank Rate on the amount by which the amount actually
maintained falls short of the prescribed minimum on that day and if the shortfall
continues on the next succeeding day/s, penal interest shall be recovered at the rate
of five per cent per annum above the Bank Rate.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be recovered from rrbs in the event of shortfall in maintenance of prescribed crr on a daily basis for that day at the rate of three percent per annum

controls.control_objective: To ensure to furnish the particulars such as date, amount, percentage, reason for default in maintenance of requisite crr and also action taken to avoid recurre
controls.control_description:
Banks are required to furnish the particulars such as date, amount, percentage,
reason for default in maintenance of requisite CRR and also action taken to avoid
recurrence of such default.
controls.implementation_method: Implement reporting procedure to to furnish the particulars such as date, amount, percentage, reason for default in maintenance of requisite crr and also action taken to avoid recurre

controls.control_objective: To ensure be punishable with fine which may extend to ₹500 and with a further fine which may extend to ₹500 for each subsequent fortnight during which default c
controls.control_description:
Under the provisions of Section 42(3A) of the RBI Act, 1934, penal interest at the
increased rate of five percent above the Bank Rate become payable and if the default
still continues during the next succeeding fortnight,
(1) Every Director, Manager or Secretary of the scheduled bank who is knowingly and
willfully a party to the default, shall be punishable with fine which may extend to ₹500
and with a further fine which may extend to ₹500 for each subsequent fortnight during
which default continues.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be punishable with fine which may extend to ₹500 and with a further fine which may extend to ₹500 for each subsequent fortnight during which default c

controls.control_objective: To ensure in respect of each such default be punishable with fine which may extend to ₹500 and with a further fine which may extend to ₹500 for each day after t
controls.control_description:
(2) The Reserve Bank may prohibit a scheduled bank from receiving any fresh deposit
after the said fortnight, and if default is made by the bank in complying with the
prohibition referred to in this clause, every director and officer of the bank who is
knowingly and willfully a party to such default or who through negligence or otherwise
contributes to such default shall in respect of each such default be punishable with
fine which may extend to ₹500 and with a further fine which may extend to ₹500 for
each day after the first, on which a deposit received in contravention of such prohibition
<image>
is retained by the scheduled bank.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to in respect of each such default be punishable with fine which may extend to ₹500 and with a further fine which may extend to ₹500 for each day after t

controls.control_objective: To ensure attract the provisions of section 42(4) of rbi act, 1934 and banks are liable for imposition of penalties as indicated therein
controls.control_description:
Failure to submit the Return/late submission of the Return shall attract the
provisions of Section 42(4) of RBI Act, 1934 and banks are liable for imposition of
penalties as indicated therein.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to attract the provisions of section 42(4) of rbi act, 1934 and banks are liable for imposition of penalties as indicated therein

controls.control_objective: To ensure be liable to pay to the reserve bank in respect of that default, the penal interest as envisaged under section 24 of the br act, 1949
controls.control_description:
On the failure of the bank to maintain as on any day, the amount of SLR required
to be maintained by a bank, the bank shall be liable to pay to the Reserve Bank in
respect of that default, the penal interest as envisaged under Section 24 of the BR Act,
1949.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be liable to pay to the reserve bank in respect of that default, the penal interest as envisaged under section 24 of the br act, 1949

controls.control_objective: To ensure be constrained to consider cancelling the licence in case of licensed banks
controls.control_description:
Where it is observed that banks are persistently defaulting despite instructions and
repeated advice, the Reserve Bank in addition to levy of penalty on such defaulting
banks, may be constrained to consider cancelling the licence in case of licensed
banks.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be constrained to consider cancelling the licence in case of licensed banks

controls.control_objective: To ensure , therefore, in their own interest ensure maintenance of statutory liquidity ratio at prescribed rates and be very prompt in submission of required re
controls.control_description:
The banks should, therefore, in their own interest ensure maintenance of
statutory liquidity ratio at prescribed rates and be very prompt in submission of
required Return to Regional Office concerned of Reserve Bank.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to , therefore, in their own interest ensure maintenance of statutory liquidity ratio at prescribed rates and be very prompt in submission of required re

controls.control_objective: To ensure be construed as a permission to undertake all such activities by a banking entity
controls.control_description:
Disclaimer: It is hereby advised that mere inclusion of any item in the above Master
Direction should not be construed as a permission to undertake all such activities
by a banking entity.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be construed as a permission to undertake all such activities by a banking entity

controls.control_objective: To ensure continue to remain repealed
controls.control_description:
The Directions,
instructions and guidelines repealed prior to the issuance of these Directions shall
continue to remain repealed.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to continue to remain repealed

controls.control_objective: To ensure continue to be governed by the provisions thereof
controls.control_description:
Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken,
or initiated under the repealed Directions, instructions, or guidelines shall continue to
be governed by the provisions thereof.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to continue to be governed by the provisions thereof

controls.control_objective: To ensure be deemed as governed by these directions
controls.control_description:
All approvals or acknowledgments granted
under these repealed lists shall be deemed as governed by these Directions.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be deemed as governed by these directions

controls.control_objective: To ensure in any way prejudicially affect:
controls.control_description:
Further,
the repeal of these directions, instructions, or guidelines shall not in any way
prejudicially affect:
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to in any way prejudicially affect:

controls.control_objective: To ensure be instituted, continued, or enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guideline
controls.control_description:
and any such investigation, legal proceedings or remedy may be instituted,
continued, or enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may be
imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guidelines had not been
repealed.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be instituted, continued, or enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guideline

controls.control_objective: To ensure be in addition to, and not in derogation of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time being in force
controls.control_description:
The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation of
the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time being in
force
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be in addition to, and not in derogation of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time being in force

controls.control_objective: To ensure be final and binding
controls.control_description:
For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions or in order to
remove any difficulties in the application or interpretation of the provisions of these
Directions, the RBI may, if it considers necessary, issue necessary clarifications in
<image>
respect of any matter covered herein and the interpretation of any provision of these
Directions given by the RBI shall be final and binding.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be final and binding

controls.control_objective: To ensure give the preceding working day's figure in respect of such office or offices, but shall nevertheless be deemed to relate to last day of that fortnight
controls.control_description:
Savings Bank Account (vide Regulation 7)
i) Demand Liabilities in India
ii) Time Liabilities in India
Place:
Date:
1 Where last day of the fortnightis a public holiday under the Negotiable Instrument Act, 1881 (26 of
1881) for one or more offices of a Scheduled bank, the return shall give the preceding working day's
figure in respect of such office or offices, but shall nevertheless be deemed to relate to last day of that
fortnight.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to give the preceding working day's figure in respect of such office or offices, but shall nevertheless be deemed to relate to last day of that fortnight

controls.control_objective: To ensure the banks and any other financial institutions referred to in sub-clause (i) to (vi) of clause (d) of the explanation below section 42 (1) of the rese
controls.control_description:
2 The expression "Banking System" or "Banks" wherever it appears in the return means the banks and
any other financial institutions referred to in sub-clause (i) to (vi) of clause (d) of the Explanation below
Section 42 (1) of the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to the banks and any other financial institutions referred to in sub-clause (i) to (vi) of clause (d) of the explanation below section 42 (1) of the rese

controls.control_objective: To ensure be included in the figure against ii(c).in such a case, the net liability to the banking system will be worked out as the excess
controls.control_description: 4 If it is not possible to provide the figure against I(c) separately from II(c), the same may be included in the figure against II(c).In such a case, the net liability to the banking system will be worked out as the excess, if any of the aggregate of 1(a) and 1(b) over the aggregate of III.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to be included in the figure against ii(c).in such a case, the net liability to the banking system will be worked out as the excess

controls.control_objective: To ensure to be maintained as per current rbi instructions under section 42 and 42(1a) of the reserve bank of india act, 1934
controls.control_description:
Any other liability on which CRR is required to be maintained as per current
RBI instructions under section 42 and 42(1A) of the Reserve Bank of India Act,
1934.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to to be maintained as per current rbi instructions under section 42 and 42(1a) of the reserve bank of india act, 1934

controls.control_objective: To ensure mean the state bank of india, corresponding new banks, regional rural banks, other banking companies, co- operative banks and financial institutions n
controls.control_description:
XIII-XI
(Excess+, deficit-)
Dated Signature
Note: For the purposes of this return, the expressions "Banking System" shall mean the State Bank of India, corresponding new banks, Regional Rural Banks, other banking companies, co- operative banks and financial institutions notified by the Central Government under clause (d) of the Explanation to section 18 of the Banking Regulation Act,1949.
controls.implementation_method: Implement operational procedure to mean the state bank of india, corresponding new banks, regional rural banks, other banking companies, co- operative banks and financial institutions n
